generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Prioritize resolving identifier references and type mismatches reported by the AGREE validator.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Ensure correct scalar type declarations (int, real, bool) in AGREE.
Use `eq` helpers to define reusable expressions for complex conditions.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce unqualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Avoid modifying the AADL architecture to resolve AGREE validation errors; focus on contract corrections.
Do not attempt to define AGREE contracts outside of a valid `annex agree` block.
